pattern: Work backwards: repeatedly find a window of target that matches stamp (possibly with already-wildcarded positions), blank those positions out to '?', and record the index; reverse the recorded indices.
hints: Reverse the process: instead of stamping '?' to characters, convert characters back to '?'. Find any window in target that matches stamp when '?' positions are ignored, blank those positions, and record the stamp index.
Repeat until all characters are '?'. Each successful match removes at least one new character. If no match is possible but target is not all '?' yet, return [].
Reverse the collected indices at the end to get the forward stamping order.

edge_inputs.description: stamp equals target — one stamp at index 0
stamp length 1, target all same char
stamp same length as target, single char each
stamp must be applied more than once with overlap
impossible-looking but solvable with partial overlap wildcard

stress.description: constructed solvable input: build target by random stamping then verify
